purpose: Automatically inspect pull-request checks and promote eligible draft pull requests to ready-for-review without routing routine lifecycle work through the operator.
requiredGateDefinition: Only checks belonging to requiredWorkflowNames determine automatic readiness. Unlisted external checks and advisory platform/game workflows are reported but do not block readiness.

alwaysAdvisoryByDefault: Windows PowerShell 5.1 compatibility runs that require a Windows runner
advisoryValidation.reason: The product and development harness must remain modular across operating systems. A game-backed run on one installed system provides useful evidence but must not become the universal PR readiness or merge gate.
requiredInstead: cross-platform builds where the project supports them
advisoryValidation.workflowRule: Keep installed-game and OS-specific jobs in separately named advisory workflows. Do not place them in a required workflow whose completion controls readiness.

draftControl.meaning: An explicitly labeled PR remains draft even after all required workflows pass. Without this label, eligible draft PRs are promoted automatically.

allowedActions: mark an eligible draft pull request ready for review
leave an already-ready pull request unchanged
forbiddenActions: commit or push unrelated repository changes
run untrusted pull-request code with a write-capable token
claim runtime or gameplay proof from readiness automation
authorizationRule: When a sprint explicitly authorizes repository changes, the agent executes its bounded commits and pushes. This lifecycle workflow itself never invents authorization for merge, close, branch deletion, force-push, history rewrite, or unrelated commits.

notReachedByAutomation: merge readiness under every branch-protection rule
build proof unless a required build workflow passed
